markdown:
#  Edit a Change Order 
##  Background 
The Project level Change Orders tool was designed to provide you with quick access to your project's change orders:
  * The 'Prime Contracts' tab lists any prime contracts with change orders on your project.
  * The 'Commitments' tab list any purchase orders or subcontracts with change orders on your project.


If you want to edit a change order, you can click its link in the 'Contract' or '#' column of either tab. This opens the contract in view mode in the appropriate Project Financials tool.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * _To edit a Commitment Change Order (CCO)_ , 'Admin' level permissions on the project's Commitments tool.  
OR
    * _To edit a Client Contract Change Order, Prime Contract Change Order, or Funding Change Order_ , 'Admin' level permissions on the project's Client Contracts, Prime Contracts, or Funding tool. See [What tool names and terms are different in Procore for general contractors, owners, and specialty contractors?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-tool-names-and-terms-are-different-in-procore-for-general-contractors-owners-and-specialty-contractors)  
AND
    * _To gain access to view change orders in the project's Change Orders tool_ , you'll also need the permissions documented in [View Change Orders](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-orders-project/tutorials/view-change-orders). 
#####  Note
User permissions for editing change orders are NOT managed by the project's Change Orders tool. Instead, the Change Orders tool is designed for users with 'Admin' level permissions on the Client Contracts, Prime Contracts, or Funding tool AND the project's Commitments tool. It allows authorized users to view and access change orders from one place. The settings below determine your ability to edit change orders:
      * Your user account's permissions on the project's Commitments and/or the Client Contracts, Prime Contracts, or Funding tool.
      * Your membership is on the 'Private' list on the specific contract or funding.
      * You also may not be able to edit certain fields, depending on the change order's status and/or other items associated with the change. To learn more, see [Why can't I edit or delete an 'Approved' change order?](https://support.procore.com/faq/why-cant-i-edit-or-delete-an-approved-change-order)
To learn more about permissions for specific tasks that can be performed with the Change Orders tool, see [Permissions: Change Orders](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-orders-project/permissions).
  * **Additional Information:**
    * When editing a change order you will be redirected to either the project's Prime Contracts tool or the Commitments tool.
    * You can also edit a change order from a prime contract, purchase order, or subcontract using the Change Orders tab under the Commitments and Prime Contracts tool. 
#####  Tip
**Need to edit a change order that is NOT the most recently approved?** If so, find the most recently approved change order. Then, change each change order's status to 'Draft' and work backward until you locate the change order that you need to edit. To learn more, see [Determine the Order in Which Change Orders were Approved](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-orders-project/tutorials/determine-the-order-in-which-change-orders-were-approved).


##  Prerequisites 
  * [Create a Commitment Change Order (CCO)](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/materials-user-guide-with-financials/create-a-change-order-cco)  
OR
  * [Create a Prime Contract Change Order](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/prime-contracts-project/tutorials/create-a-prime-contract-change-order)


##  Steps 
  * Select the Change Order to Edit
  * Edit a Commitment Change Order
  * Edit a Prime Contract Change Order


### Select the Change Order to Edit
  1. Navigate to the project's **Change Orders** tool.
  2. Click the **Prime Contracts** or **Commitments** tab.
  3. Locate the change order to edit. Then click **View.**  
This opens the purchase order, subcontract, or prime contract in the appropriate tool.
  4. Follow the appropriate steps:
     * Edit a Commitment Change Order
     * Edit a Prime Contract Change Order


### Edit a Commitment Change Order
Follow these steps to edit a change order for a purchase order or subcontract.
  1. In the contract, click the **Change Orders** tab.
  2. In the 'Commitment Change Orders' table, click the **Edit** button.
  3. Modify the change order's data as needed. To learn about the different fields, see [Create](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/materials-user-guide-with-financials/create-a-change-order-cco)[ a Commitment Change Order](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/materials-user-guide-with-financials/create-a-change-order-cco).


### Edit a Prime Contract Change Order
Follow these steps to edit a change order for a prime contract.
  1. In the change order, click the **Edit** button.
  2. Modify the change order's data as needed. To learn about the different fields, see or [Create a Prime Contract Change Order](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/prime-contracts-project/tutorials/create-a-prime-contract-change-order).


## See Also
  * [Can I create a change order for a contract or funding before a downstream collaborator responds to an RFQ for a change event?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-can-i-create-a-change-order-for-a-contract-or-funding-before-a-downstream-collaborator-responds-to-an-rfq-for-a-change-event)
  * [What granular permissions are available for the project's Change Orders tool?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-granular-permissions-are-available-for-the-projects-change-orders-tool)
  * [Approve or Reject Commitment Change Orders](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-orders-project/tutorials/approve-or-reject-commitment-change-orders)
title: Edit a Change Order
description: How to edit a change order.
